title: Vecs 2
description: You can iterate over vectors and modify their elements. This exercise explores both approaches.
instructions: Complete the two functions to transform vectors by multiplying each element by 2.
hints: For the loop version, `element` is a mutable reference. Use `*element` to dereference it.
For the map version, `element` is a reference. Multiply and return: `element * 2`.
explanation: There are two main approaches to transforming vectors: imperative (using a loop with `iter_mut()`) and functional (using `iter().map().collect()`). With `iter_mut()`, you get mutable references and must dereference with `*` to modify values in place. With `map()`, you create a new vector from transformed values.
